---
title: "Findability and publication count of Swiss HEIs from the TOBI project"
format:
  dashboard:
    css: ../dashboard-common.css
author: "Dr Moritz Mähr"
description: "Findability and output coverage of Swiss Higher Education Institutions in five bibliometric databases, based on TOBI project data."
image: images/dashboard-screenshot.png
image-alt: "Screenshot of the TOBI dashboard with value boxes and summary tables"
image-caption: "Dashboard screenshot from the NAIF website, captured on 2026-03-04."
date: today
draft: false
resources:
  - tobi.csv
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import html

import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import HTML, display

df = pd.read_csv("tobi.csv")

TOBI_SOURCES = [
    (
        "Dimensions",
        "tobi_findable_dim",
        "tobi_total_outputs_dim_2012_2022",
        "tobi_retrieval_date_dim",
        "https://www.dimensions.ai/",
    ),
    (
        "Scopus",
        "tobi_findable_scopus",
        "tobi_total_outputs_scopus_2012_2022",
        "tobi_retrieval_date_scopus",
        "https://www.scopus.com/",
    ),
    (
        "Web of Science",
        "tobi_findable_wos",
        "tobi_total_outputs_wos_2012_2022",
        "tobi_retrieval_date_wos",
        "https://www.webofscience.com/",
    ),
    (
        "OpenAIRE",
        "tobi_findable_openaire",
        "tobi_total_outputs_openaire_2012_2022",
        "tobi_retrieval_date_openaire",
        "https://www.openaire.eu/",
    ),
    (
        "OpenAlex",
        "tobi_findable_openalex",
        "tobi_total_outputs_openalex_2012_2022",
        "tobi_retrieval_date_openalex",
        "https://openalex.org/",
    ),
]

TYPE_LABELS = {
    "University": "Universities",
    "Univ. Inst.": "University institutes",
    "UAS": "Universities of applied sciences (UAS)",
    "UAS Inst.": "UAS institutes",
    "UTE": "Teacher education institutions (UTE)",
}

TYPE_ORDER = ["University", "Univ. Inst.", "UAS", "UAS Inst.", "UTE"]


def normalise_yes_no(value: object) -> str:
    """Normalise status values to Yes/No/Unknown."""
    if pd.isna(value):
        return "Unknown"
    text = str(value).strip().lower()
    if text in {"yes", "true", "1"}:
        return "Yes"
    if text in {"no", "false", "0"}:
        return "No"
    return "Unknown"


def format_identifier(value: object) -> str:
    """Convert IDs to stable strings and remove float artefacts."""
    if pd.isna(value):
        return ""
    text = str(value).strip()
    if not text:
        return ""
    if text.endswith(".0"):
        try:
            numeric_value = float(text)
            if numeric_value.is_integer():
                return str(int(numeric_value))
        except ValueError:
            return text
    return text


def format_plain_text(value: object) -> str:
    """Return plain text values with a fallback dash."""
    text = format_identifier(value)
    return text if text else "-"


def format_identifier_list(value: object) -> str:
    """Render pipe-separated identifier lists with soft wrap points."""
    text = format_identifier(value)
    if not text:
        return "-"
    parts = [html.escape(part.strip()) for part in text.split("|") if part.strip()]
    if not parts:
        return "-"
    return (
        '<span class="dashboard-id-list">'
        + '<wbr><span class="dashboard-id-sep">|</span><wbr>'.join(
            f'<span class="dashboard-id-token">{part}</span>' for part in parts
        )
        + "</span>"
    )


def format_type_label(value: object) -> str:
    """Return the full label for an institution type."""
    text = format_identifier(value)
    if not text:
        return "Unknown"
    return TYPE_LABELS.get(text, text)


def type_order_key(value: object) -> int:
    """Return a stable sort key for institution types."""
    text = format_identifier(value)
    try:
        return TYPE_ORDER.index(text)
    except ValueError:
        return len(TYPE_ORDER)


def pct(value: int, denominator: int) -> str:
    """Format a value as a percentage string."""
    if denominator == 0:
        return "0.0%"
    return f"{(100 * value / denominator):.1f}%"


def pct_float(value: int, denominator: int) -> float:
    """Return percentage as a float."""
    if denominator == 0:
        return 0.0
    return 100 * value / denominator


def heatmap_colour(percentage: float) -> str:
    """Return a background colour interpolated from red (0%) to green (100%)."""
    clamped = max(0.0, min(100.0, percentage))
    ratio = clamped / 100.0

    # Interpolate from muted red (#fecaca) via muted yellow (#fef9c3) to muted green (#bbf7d0)
    if ratio < 0.5:
        t = ratio / 0.5
        r = int(254 + t * (254 - 254))
        g = int(202 + t * (249 - 202))
        b = int(202 + t * (195 - 202))
    else:
        t = (ratio - 0.5) / 0.5
        r = int(254 + t * (187 - 254))
        g = int(249 + t * (247 - 249))
        b = int(195 + t * (208 - 195))

    return f"#{r:02x}{g:02x}{b:02x}"


def pct_cell(value: int, denominator: int) -> str:
    """Format a percentage with heatmap background colour."""
    percentage = pct_float(value, denominator)
    colour = heatmap_colour(percentage)
    text = f"{percentage:.1f}%"
    return (
        f'<span style="display:inline-block; padding:2px 8px; border-radius:4px; '
        f'background:{colour}; white-space:nowrap;">{text}</span>'
    )


def make_link(url: str, label: str) -> str:
    """Create a safe external link."""
    return f'<a href="{url}" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">{html.escape(label)}</a>'


def display_html_table(dataframe: pd.DataFrame) -> None:
    """Render a dataframe in a scrollable table container."""
    table_html = dataframe.to_html(
        escape=False,
        index=False,
        classes="table table-sm table-striped align-middle",
        border=0,
    )
    display(HTML(f'<div class="dashboard-table-wrap">{table_html}</div>'))


for _, findability_column, output_column, _, _ in TOBI_SOURCES:
    if findability_column in df.columns:
        df[findability_column] = df[findability_column].map(normalise_yes_no)
    if output_column in df.columns:
        df[output_column] = pd.to_numeric(df[output_column], errors="coerce")

findability_columns = [c for _, c, _, _, _ in TOBI_SOURCES if c in df.columns]
output_columns = [c for _, _, c, _, _ in TOBI_SOURCES if c in df.columns]

total = len(df)
any_tobi = int(
    (
        df[findability_columns].ne("Unknown").any(axis=1)
        | df[output_columns].notna().any(axis=1)
    ).sum()
)

openalex_findable = int(df["tobi_findable_openalex"].eq("Yes").sum())
dimensions_findable = int(df["tobi_findable_dim"].eq("Yes").sum())
openalex_outputs = int(df["tobi_total_outputs_openalex_2012_2022"].fillna(0).sum())
```

## Row {height=100%}

### Column {width=28%}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "HEIs with TOBI data"
dict(icon="database", color="light", value=pct(any_tobi, total))
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "OpenAlex findable"
dict(icon="search", color="light", value=pct(openalex_findable, total))
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Dimensions findable"
dict(icon="diagram-3", color="light", value=pct(dimensions_findable, total))
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "OpenAlex outputs"
dict(icon="bar-chart", color="light", value=f"{openalex_outputs:,}")
```

### Column {.tabset}

#### About

```{python}
#| echo: false
intro_html = f"""
<div class="dashboard-about">
  <p>
    This dashboard presents data from the
    <strong><a href="https://eth-library.github.io/tobi/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">TOBI project</a></strong>
    (Towards Open Bibliometric Indicators) on the findability and publication output of
    <strong>{total}</strong> Swiss Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) in five bibliometric
    databases: Dimensions, Scopus, Web of Science, OpenAIRE, and OpenAlex.
  </p>
  <p>
    The TOBI project, conducted at ETH Library, investigated how well Swiss HEIs are
    represented in commercial and open bibliometric databases. Its findings are particularly
    relevant for research assessment and the development of open bibliometric indicators.
  </p>

  <h5 class="dashboard-subheading" style="margin-top: 1.5rem;">Institution types</h5>
  <table class="table table-sm" style="max-width: 52ch;">
    <thead><tr><th>Abbreviation</th><th>Full name</th></tr></thead>
    <tbody>
      <tr><td><strong>University</strong></td><td>Cantonal universities and federal institutes of technology</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>Univ.&nbsp;Inst.</strong></td><td>University institutes</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>UAS</strong></td><td>Universities of applied sciences</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>UAS&nbsp;Inst.</strong></td><td>UAS institutes</td></tr>
      <tr><td><strong>UTE</strong></td><td>Teacher education institutions</td></tr>
    </tbody>
  </table>

  <h5 class="dashboard-subheading" style="margin-top: 1.5rem;">Further reading</h5>
  <ul>
    <li>
      <a href="https://yearofscientometrics.ethz.ch/exploring-swiss-higher-education-institutions-in-commercial-and-open-bibliometric-databases/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">
        Exploring Swiss Higher Education Institutions in Commercial and Open Bibliometric Databases
      </a> &ndash; Swiss Year of Scientometrics (SYoS)
    </li>
    <li>
      <a href="../../posts/2026-03-25-naif-continues-tobi/">
        How NAIF builds on TOBI's results in Switzerland
      </a> &ndash; NAIF blog
    </li>
    <li>
      Dederke, J., Koch, M., &amp; Willemin, S. (2024).
      <em>The Representation of Swiss Higher Education Institutions in Five Bibliometric Databases</em>.
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.3929/ethz-b-000726102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">https://doi.org/10.3929/ethz-b-000726102</a>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>
"""
display(HTML(intro_html))
```

#### Findability of institutions

```{python}
#| echo: false
findability_rows = []
for source, findability_column, _, retrieval_column, source_url in TOBI_SOURCES:
    yes_count = int(df[findability_column].eq("Yes").sum())
    no_count = int(df[findability_column].eq("No").sum())
    unknown_count = int(df[findability_column].eq("Unknown").sum())
    known_count = yes_count + no_count

    latest_retrieval = pd.to_datetime(df[retrieval_column], errors="coerce").dropna().max()
    latest_retrieval_text = (
        latest_retrieval.strftime("%Y-%m-%d") if pd.notna(latest_retrieval) else "-"
    )

    findability_rows.append(
        [
            make_link(source_url, source),
            yes_count,
            no_count,
            unknown_count,
            pct_cell(yes_count, total),
            pct_cell(yes_count, known_count) if known_count else "-",
            latest_retrieval_text,
        ]
    )

findability_summary = pd.DataFrame(
    findability_rows,
    columns=[
        "Source",
        "Findable",
        "Not findable",
        "Unknown",
        "Findable share (all HEIs)",
        "Findable share (known only)",
        "Latest retrieval",
    ],
)
display_html_table(findability_summary)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
type_findability_rows = []
for institution_type in TYPE_ORDER:
    group = df[df["type"] == institution_type]
    if group.empty:
        continue
    row: dict[str, object] = {
        "Type": format_type_label(institution_type),
        "Institutions": len(group),
    }
    for source, findability_column, _, _, _ in TOBI_SOURCES:
        findable_count = int(group[findability_column].eq("Yes").sum())
        row[f"{source} findable"] = findable_count
        row[f"{source} %"] = pct_cell(findable_count, len(group))
    type_findability_rows.append(row)

# Add any types not in TYPE_ORDER
for institution_type, group in df.groupby("type", dropna=False):
    if institution_type in TYPE_ORDER:
        continue
    row = {
        "Type": format_type_label(institution_type),
        "Institutions": len(group),
    }
    for source, findability_column, _, _, _ in TOBI_SOURCES:
        findable_count = int(group[findability_column].eq("Yes").sum())
        row[f"{source} findable"] = findable_count
        row[f"{source} %"] = pct_cell(findable_count, len(group))
    type_findability_rows.append(row)

type_findability = pd.DataFrame(type_findability_rows)
display_html_table(type_findability.reset_index(drop=True))
```

#### Publication counts (2012–2022)

```{python}
#| echo: false
output_rows = []
for source, _, output_column, retrieval_column, source_url in TOBI_SOURCES:
    values = df[output_column]
    institutions_with_values = int(values.notna().sum())
    total_outputs = int(values.fillna(0).sum())
    median_outputs = int(values.dropna().median()) if institutions_with_values else 0

    latest_retrieval = pd.to_datetime(df[retrieval_column], errors="coerce").dropna().max()
    latest_retrieval_text = (
        latest_retrieval.strftime("%Y-%m-%d") if pd.notna(latest_retrieval) else "-"
    )

    output_rows.append(
        [
            make_link(source_url, source),
            institutions_with_values,
            f"{total_outputs:,}",
            f"{median_outputs:,}",
            latest_retrieval_text,
        ]
    )

output_summary = pd.DataFrame(
    output_rows,
    columns=[
        "Source",
        "HEIs with output data",
        "Total outputs",
        "Median outputs per HEI",
        "Latest retrieval",
    ],
)
display_html_table(output_summary)
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
output_column_names = [output_column for _, _, output_column, _, _ in TOBI_SOURCES]
output_rename = {
    "tobi_total_outputs_dim_2012_2022": "Dimensions",
    "tobi_total_outputs_scopus_2012_2022": "Scopus",
    "tobi_total_outputs_wos_2012_2022": "Web of Science",
    "tobi_total_outputs_openaire_2012_2022": "OpenAIRE",
    "tobi_total_outputs_openalex_2012_2022": "OpenAlex",
}

top_outputs = df[["name", "short_name", "type", *output_column_names]].copy()
top_outputs["Largest source count"] = top_outputs[output_column_names].max(
    axis=1, skipna=True
)
top_outputs = top_outputs[top_outputs["Largest source count"].notna()]
top_outputs = top_outputs.sort_values("Largest source count", ascending=False).head(15)
top_outputs["type"] = top_outputs["type"].apply(format_type_label)
top_outputs = top_outputs.rename(
    columns={
        "name": "Institution",
        "short_name": "Short",
        "type": "Type",
        **output_rename,
    }
)

for column in [
    "Dimensions",
    "Scopus",
    "Web of Science",
    "OpenAIRE",
    "OpenAlex",
    "Largest source count",
]:
    top_outputs[column] = top_outputs[column].map(
        lambda value: f"{int(value):,}" if pd.notna(value) else "-"
    )

display_html_table(top_outputs.reset_index(drop=True))
```

#### List of institutions

```{python}
#| echo: false
all_institutions = df[
    [
        "name",
        "short_name",
        "type",
        "town",
        "swissuniversities_name",
        "openalex_ids",
        "openorgs_ids",
        "tobi_findable_dim",
        "tobi_findable_scopus",
        "tobi_findable_wos",
        "tobi_findable_openaire",
        "tobi_findable_openalex",
        "tobi_total_outputs_openalex_2012_2022",
    ]
].copy()

all_institutions["type"] = all_institutions["type"].apply(format_type_label)
all_institutions["town"] = all_institutions["town"].apply(format_plain_text)
all_institutions["swissuniversities_name"] = all_institutions["swissuniversities_name"].apply(
    format_plain_text
)
all_institutions["openalex_ids"] = all_institutions["openalex_ids"].apply(
    format_identifier_list
)
all_institutions["openorgs_ids"] = all_institutions["openorgs_ids"].apply(
    format_identifier_list
)

all_institutions["tobi_total_outputs_openalex_2012_2022"] = all_institutions[
    "tobi_total_outputs_openalex_2012_2022"
].map(lambda value: f"{int(value):,}" if pd.notna(value) else "-")

all_institutions["type_order"] = all_institutions["type"].apply(type_order_key)

all_institutions = all_institutions.rename(
    columns={
        "name": "Institution",
        "short_name": "Short",
        "type": "Type",
        "town": "Town",
        "swissuniversities_name": "swissuniversities",
        "openalex_ids": "OpenAlex IDs",
        "openorgs_ids": "OpenOrgs IDs",
        "tobi_findable_dim": "Dim.",
        "tobi_findable_scopus": "Scopus",
        "tobi_findable_wos": "WoS",
        "tobi_findable_openaire": "OpenAIRE",
        "tobi_findable_openalex": "OpenAlex",
        "tobi_total_outputs_openalex_2012_2022": "OpenAlex outputs (2012–2022)",
    }
)

all_institutions = all_institutions.sort_values(
    ["type_order", "Town", "Institution"]
).reset_index(drop=True)

all_institutions_overview = all_institutions[
    [
        "Institution",
        "Short",
        "Type",
        "Town",
        "Dim.",
        "Scopus",
        "WoS",
        "OpenAIRE",
        "OpenAlex",
        "OpenAlex outputs (2012–2022)",
    ]
].copy()

all_institutions_identifiers = all_institutions[
    [
        "Institution",
        "Type",
        "swissuniversities",
        "OpenAlex IDs",
        "OpenOrgs IDs",
    ]
].copy()

display(HTML('<h5 class="dashboard-subheading">Overview</h5>'))
display_html_table(all_institutions_overview)
display(HTML('<h5 class="dashboard-subheading">Identifiers</h5>'))
display_html_table(all_institutions_identifiers)
```

#### Data sources and credits

```{python}
#| echo: false
credits_html = """
<div class="dashboard-about">
  <p>
    This dashboard reuses TOBI project data on Swiss Higher Education Institutions and
    presents it in a compact NAIF format.
  </p>

  <h5 class="dashboard-subheading" style="margin-top: 1.5rem;">Data basis</h5>
  <ul>
    <li>
      Dederke, J., Koch, M., &amp; Willemin, S. (2024).
      <em>The Representation of Swiss Higher Education Institutions in Five Bibliometric Databases</em>.
      <a href="https://doi.org/10.3929/ethz-b-000726102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">https://doi.org/10.3929/ethz-b-000726102</a>
    </li>
    <li>
      Project context: TOBI (Towards Open Bibliometric Indicators), ETH Library.
      <a href="https://eth-library.github.io/tobi/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">https://eth-library.github.io/tobi/</a>
    </li>
  </ul>

  <h5 class="dashboard-subheading" style="margin-top: 1.5rem;">Data providers</h5>
  <ul>
    <li><a href="https://www.dimensions.ai/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Dimensions</a> (Digital Science)</li>
    <li><a href="https://www.scopus.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Scopus</a> (Elsevier)</li>
    <li><a href="https://www.webofscience.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Web of Science</a> (Clarivate)</li>
    <li><a href="https://www.openaire.eu/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">OpenAIRE</a></li>
    <li><a href="https://openalex.org/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">OpenAlex</a> (OurResearch)</li>
  </ul>

  <h5 class="dashboard-subheading" style="margin-top: 1.5rem;">Dashboard</h5>
  <p>
    Adaptation and maintenance: Dr Moritz Mähr
    (<a href="https://eth-library.github.io/naif/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">NAIF project</a>).
  </p>
</div>
"""
display(HTML(credits_html))
```
